functions.name: between
functions.description: Checks if a value is between a minimum and maximum value (inclusive).
functions.example_text: For example, between(5, 1, 10) would return True.
functions.returns: True if the value is between min and max (inclusive), False otherwise

functions.name: coalesce
functions.description: Returns the first non-null value from a list of values.
functions.example_text: For example, coalesce(null, null, "default") would return "default".

parameters.name: *values
parameters.description: Multiple values to check in order

functions.returns: The first non-null value, or null if all values are null

functions.name: contains
functions.description: Checks if some text contains a specific pattern.
functions.example_text: For example, contains("hello world", "world") would return True.
functions.returns: True if the pattern is found in the text, False otherwise

functions.name: does_not_equal
functions.description: Checks if two values are different from each other.
functions.example_text: For example, does_not_equal("apple", "orange") would return True.
functions.returns: True if the values are different, False if they're the same

functions.name: equals
functions.description: Checks if two values are equal to each other.
functions.example_text: For example, equals("apple", "apple") would return True.
functions.returns: True if the values are equal, False otherwise

functions.name: greatest
functions.description: Returns the largest value from a list of values.

functions.name: ifnull
functions.description: Returns a default value if the input is null.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: The value to return if the first value is null

functions.returns: The original value if not null, otherwise the default value

functions.name: is_empty
functions.description: Checks if a value is empty (null/missing).
functions.example_text: For example, is_empty(null) would return True.
functions.returns: True if the value is empty, False otherwise

functions.name: is_not_empty
functions.description: Checks if a value contains something (is not null/missing).
functions.example_text: For example, is_not_empty("apple") would return True.
functions.returns: True if the value contains something, False if it's empty

functions.name: is_string
functions.description: Checks if a value is text (a string).
functions.example_text: For example, is_string("apple") would return True, but is_string(123) would return False.
functions.example_result: True, but is_string(123) would return False
functions.returns: True if the value is text, False otherwise

functions.name: least
functions.description: Returns the smallest value from a list of values.

functions.name: nullif
functions.description: Returns null if the two values are equal, otherwise returns the first value.
functions.example_text: For example, nullif(5, 5) would return null, but nullif(5, 3) would return 5.
functions.returns: null if value1 equals value2, otherwise value1

functions.name: nvl
functions.description: Returns a default value if the input is null (alias for ifnull).

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: The value to return if the first value is null

functions.returns: The original value if not null, otherwise the default value

functions.name: concat
functions.description: Combines multiple text values into a single text.
functions.example_text: For example, concat("Hello", " ", "World") would return "Hello World".

parameters.name: *text_parts
parameters.description: The texts you want to combine

functions.name: count_match
functions.description: Counts how many times a pattern appears in text.

functions.name: ends_with
functions.description: Checks if text ends with a specific suffix.
functions.example_text: For example, ends_with("Hello World", "World") would return True.

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: The suffix to look for at the end

functions.returns: True if the text ends with the suffix, False otherwise

functions.name: find_position
functions.description: Find the position of a substring within a string.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text in which to find the position of the substring. Can be an expression or any other value.

functions.name: left
functions.description: Gets a specified number of characters from the beginning of text.
functions.example_text: For example, left("Hello World", 5) would return "Hello".

parameters.name: num_chars
parameters.description: How many characters to take from the beginning

functions.name: left_trim
functions.description: Removes spaces from the beginning of text.
functions.example_text: For example, left_trim("  hello world  ") would return "hello world  ".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text you want to trim

functions.name: length
functions.description: Counts the number of characters in text.

functions.name: lowercase
functions.description: Converts text to all lowercase.
functions.example_text: For example, lowercase("Hello World") would return "hello world".

functions.name: mid
functions.description: Extracts a portion of text from the middle starting at a specified position.
functions.example_text: For example, mid("Hello World", 0, 5) would return "Hello".

functions.name: pad_left
functions.description: Adds characters to the beginning of text to reach a specific length.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text you want to pad

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How long you want the final text to be

parameters.name: pad_character
parameters.description: What character to use for padding (default is a space)

functions.name: pad_right
functions.description: Adds characters to the end of text to reach a specific length.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text you want to pad

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: How long you want the final text to be

parameters.name: pad_character
parameters.description: What character to use for padding (default is a space)

functions.name: repeat
functions.description: Repeats text a specified number of times.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: How many times to repeat the text

functions.name: replace
functions.description: Replaces specific text with different text.
functions.example_text: For example, replace("Hello world", "world", "friend") would return "Hello friend".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The original text where replacements will be made

parameters.name: find_text
parameters.description: The text you want to find and replace

parameters.name: replace_with
parameters.description: The new text that will replace the found text

functions.name: reverse
functions.description: Reverses the characters in text.

functions.name: right
functions.description: Gets a specified number of characters from the end of text.
functions.example_text: For example, right("Hello World", 5) would return "World".

parameters.name: num_chars
parameters.description: How many characters to take from the end

functions.name: right_trim
functions.description: Removes spaces from the end of text.
functions.example_text: For example, right_trim("hello world  ") would return "hello world".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text you want to trim

functions.name: split
functions.description: Splits text into a list using a delimiter.

functions.name: starts_with
functions.description: Checks if text starts with a specific prefix.
functions.example_text: For example, starts_with("Hello World", "Hello") would return True.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to look for at the start

functions.returns: True if the text starts with the prefix, False otherwise

functions.name: string_similarity
functions.description: Measures how similar two texts are to each other, on a scale of 0 to 1. A value of 1 means the texts are identical, while 0 means they are completely different.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Which comparison method to use (default is 'levenshtein')

functions.name: substring
functions.description: Extracts a portion of text starting at a specified position (alias for mid).
functions.example_text: For example, substring("Hello World", 6, 5) would return "World".

functions.name: titlecase
functions.description: Converts Text To Title Case, Where Each Word Is Capitalized.
functions.example_text: For example, titlecase("hello world") would return "Hello World".

functions.name: trim
functions.description: Removes spaces from both the beginning and end of text.
functions.example_text: For example, trim("  hello world  ") would return "hello world".

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text you want to trim

functions.name: uppercase
functions.description: Converts text to ALL UPPERCASE.
functions.example_text: For example, uppercase("Hello World") would return "HELLO WORLD".

functions.name: abs
functions.description: Returns the absolute value of a number (removes negative sign).
functions.example_text: For example, abs(-5) would return 5, and abs(5) would still return 5.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to get the absolute value of

functions.returns: The positive version of the number

functions.name: acos
functions.description: Calculates the arccosine (inverse cosine) of a number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the arccosine of (between -1 and 1)

functions.returns: The angle in radians whose cosine equals the input

functions.name: asin
functions.description: Calculates the arcsine (inverse sine) of a number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the arcsine of (between -1 and 1)

functions.returns: The angle in radians whose sine equals the input

functions.name: atan
functions.description: Calculates the arctangent (inverse tangent) of a number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the arctangent of

functions.returns: The angle in radians whose tangent equals the input

functions.name: ceil
functions.description: Rounds a number up to the nearest whole number.
functions.example_text: For example, ceil(4.2) would return 5, and ceil(4.9) would also return 5.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.name: cos
functions.description: Calculates the cosine of an angle (in radians).

parameters.name: angle
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.name: exp
functions.description: Calculates e raised to the power of a number.
functions.example_text: For example, exp(1) would return approximately 2.718.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The power to raise e to

functions.name: floor
functions.description: Rounds a number down to the nearest whole number.
functions.example_text: For example, floor(4.7) would return 4, and floor(4.2) would also return 4.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.name: log
functions.description: Calculates the natural logarithm of a number.
functions.example_text: For example, log(2.718) would return approximately 1.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the logarithm for

functions.returns: The natural logarithm of the number

functions.name: log10
functions.description: Calculates the base-10 logarithm of a number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the logarithm for

functions.returns: The base-10 logarithm of the number

functions.name: log2
functions.description: Calculates the base-2 logarithm of a number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the logarithm for

functions.returns: The base-2 logarithm of the number

functions.name: mod
functions.description: Calculates the remainder of division (modulo).
functions.example_text: For example, mod(10, 3) would return 1 (10 divided by 3 is 3 remainder 1).

parameters.name: dividend
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

parameters.name: divisor
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.name: negation
functions.description: Changes the sign of a number (positive becomes negative, negative becomes positive).
functions.example_text: For example, negation(5) would return -5, and negation(-3) would return 3.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to change the sign of

functions.returns: The number with its sign flipped

functions.name: negative
functions.description: Returns the value -1. This is a simple utility function that always returns negative one.

functions.name: pow
functions.description: Raises a number to a power (alias for power).

parameters.name: base
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

parameters.name: exponent
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The power to raise the base to

functions.returns: The result of base raised to the power of exponent

functions.name: power
functions.description: Raises a number to a power.

parameters.name: base
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

parameters.name: exponent
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The power to raise the base to

functions.returns: The result of base raised to the power of exponent

functions.name: random_int
functions.description: Generates a random whole number between two values.
functions.example_text: For example, random_int(1, 6) might return 4 (like rolling a die).

parameters.name: min_value
parameters.description: The smallest possible number to generate (default is 0)

parameters.name: max_value
parameters.description: The largest possible number to generate (default is 2)

functions.returns: A random number between min_value and max_value

functions.name: round
functions.description: Rounds a number to a specified number of decimal places.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

parameters.name: decimal_places
parameters.description: How many decimal places to keep (default is 0)

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Returns the sign of a number (-1, 0, or 1).
functions.example_text: For example, sign(-5) returns -1, sign(0) returns 0, sign(5) returns 1.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.returns: 1 if negative, 0 if zero, 1 if positive

functions.name: sin
functions.description: Calculates the sine of an angle (in radians).

parameters.name: angle
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.name: sqrt
functions.description: Calculates the square root of a number.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the square root of

functions.returns: The square root of the number

functions.name: tan
functions.description: Calculates the tangent of an angle (in radians).

parameters.name: angle
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})

functions.name: tanh
functions.description: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of a number. The result is always between -1 and 1.

parameters.name: number
parameters.type: DataTypeGroup({Float32, Int64, UInt128, Int16, UInt32, UInt8, Float64, Float16, Int32, UInt64, Int8, UInt16, Decimal, Int128})
parameters.description: The number to calculate the hyperbolic tangent of

functions.returns: The hyperbolic tangent of the number

functions.name: add_days
functions.description: Adds a number of days to a date.

functions.name: add_hours
functions.description: Adds a number of hours to a date and time.

functions.name: add_minutes
functions.description: Adds a number of minutes to a date and time.

functions.name: add_months
functions.description: Adds a number of months to a date.

functions.name: add_seconds
functions.description: Adds a number of seconds to a date and time.

functions.name: add_weeks
functions.description: Adds a number of weeks to a date.

functions.name: add_years
functions.description: Adds a number of years to a date.

functions.name: date_diff_days
functions.description: Calculates the number of days between two dates.
functions.returns: The number of days between the two dates

functions.name: date_trim
functions.description: Removes the smaller parts of a date or time.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date and time to trim

functions.name: date_truncate
functions.description: Rounds a date down to the nearest specified unit.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date and time to truncate

parameters.name: truncate_by
parameters.description: The time unit to round down to (like "1d", "2h", "15m")

functions.name: datetime_diff_nanoseconds
functions.description: Calculates the number of nanoseconds between two dates and times. For very precise time measurements, this function shows the exact difference down to billionths of a second.
functions.returns: The number of nanoseconds between the two dates and times

functions.name: datetime_diff_seconds
functions.description: Calculates the number of seconds between two dates and times.
functions.returns: The number of seconds between the two dates and times

functions.name: day
functions.description: Gets the day from a date.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the day from

functions.returns: The day of the month as a number (1-31)

functions.name: dayofweek
functions.description: Gets the day of the week from a date (alias for weekday).

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the day of week from

functions.returns: The day of the week as a number (1-7)

functions.name: dayofyear
functions.description: Gets the day of the year from a date (1-366).

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the day of year from

functions.returns: The day of the year as a number (1-366)

functions.name: end_of_month
functions.description: Gets the last day of the month for a given date.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to get the end of month for

functions.returns: The last day of the month

functions.name: format_date
functions.description: Formats a date as text using a specified format.

parameters.name: date_format
parameters.description: The output format string (default is year-month-day)

functions.name: hour
functions.description: Gets the hour from a time.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date and time to extract the hour from

functions.name: minute
functions.description: Gets the minute from a time.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date and time to extract the minute from

functions.name: month
functions.description: month(date_value: Any) Gets the month from a date.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the month from

functions.name: now
functions.description: Gets the current date and time.

functions.name: quarter
functions.description: Gets the quarter from a date (1-4).

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the quarter from

functions.name: second
functions.description: Gets the second from a time.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date and time to extract the second from

functions.name: start_of_month
functions.description: Gets the first day of the month for a given date.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to get the start of month for

functions.returns: The first day of the month

functions.name: today
functions.description: Gets the current date.

functions.name: week
functions.description: Gets the ISO week number from a date (1-53).

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the week from

functions.returns: The week number as a number (1-53)

functions.name: weekday
functions.description: Gets the day of the week from a date (1=Monday, 7=Sunday).

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the weekday from

functions.returns: The day of the week as a number (1-7)

functions.name: year
functions.description: Gets the year from a date.

parameters.name: date_value
parameters.description: The date to extract the year from

functions.name: to_boolean
functions.description: Converts a value to True or False.
functions.example_text: For example: - to_boolean(1) returns True - to_boolean(0) returns False - to_boolean("t") returns True - to_boolean("f") returns False - to_boolean("true") returns True - to_boolean("false") returns False - to_boolean("yes") returns True - to_boolean("no") returns False

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to convert to a boolean

functions.returns: A Polars expression that will evaluate to True or False

functions.name: to_date
functions.description: Converts text to a date value.
functions.example_text: For example, to_date("2023-01-15") would return a date value for January 15, 2023.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to convert to a date

parameters.name: date_format
parameters.description: Instructions for how to interpret the date text (default is year-month-day)

functions.name: to_datetime
functions.description: Convert a string to a datetime.
functions.returns: pl.Expr: A pl expression representing the converted datetime.

functions.name: to_decimal
functions.description: Converts a value to a decimal number with fixed precision.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to convert to a decimal

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: How many decimal places to keep (if None, keeps all decimal places)

functions.name: to_float
functions.description: Converts a value to a number with decimal places.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to convert to a floating-point number

functions.name: to_integer
functions.description: Converts a value to a whole number.
functions.example_text: For example, to_integer("123") would return 123, and to_integer(45.67) would return 45.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to convert to an integer

functions.returns: The integer value (decimal places are truncated)

functions.name: to_number
functions.description: Converts a value to a number (same as to_float).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to convert to a number

functions.name: to_string
functions.description: Converts any value to text.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to convert to text
